creator: rdna@rdna.ru
text:
>Description:

I tested the port in dirty environment so I missed one dependence.
I'm very sorry. New version of port is below.

>Fix:

--- p5-Multiplex-CMD.shar begins here ---
# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	p5-Multiplex-CMD
#	p5-Multiplex-CMD/Makefile
#	p5-Multiplex-CMD/distinfo
#	p5-Multiplex-CMD/pkg-plist
#	p5-Multiplex-CMD/pkg-descr
#
echo c - p5-Multiplex-CMD
mkdir -p p5-Multiplex-CMD > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - p5-Multiplex-CMD/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >p5-Multiplex-CMD/Makefile << '7f3339390c3cd4daf87b7c4797547dbe'
X# New ports collection makefile for:	p5-Multiplex-CMD
X# Date created:				21 March 2011
X# Whom:					Andrey Ignatov <rdna@rdna.ru>
X#
X# $FreeBSD$
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	Multiplex-CMD
XPORTVERSION=	v0.03
XCATEGORIES=	devel perl5
XMASTER_SITES=	CPAN
XPKGNAMEPREFIX=	p5-
X
XMAINTAINER=	rdna@rdna.ru
XCOMMENT=	Perl5 module which provides Multiplexed Fork Client
X
XBUILD_DEPENDS=	p5-version>=0:${PORTSDIR}/devel/p5-version
XRUN_DEPENDS=	${BUILD_DEPENDS}
X
XPERL_CONFIGURE=	yes
X
XMAN3=		Multiplex::CMD.3
X
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
7f3339390c3cd4daf87b7c4797547dbe
echo x - p5-Multiplex-CMD/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >p5-Multiplex-CMD/distinfo << '80a5e6e79f4aa27a5009258c679c2abf'
XSHA256 (Multiplex-CMD-v0.03.tar.gz) = 5faead2c523ba10c67c4562baf139b046c27d8ef03e99777dd7c561721ac89d0
XSIZE (Multiplex-CMD-v0.03.tar.gz) = 4237
80a5e6e79f4aa27a5009258c679c2abf
echo x - p5-Multiplex-CMD/pkg-plist
sed 's/^X//' >p5-Multiplex-CMD/pkg-plist << '74543a1fcea6ded5caa25840a1853142'
X%%SITE_PERL%%/Multiplex/CMD.pm
X%%SITE_PERL%%/%%PERL_ARCH%%/auto/Multiplex/CMD/.packlist
X@dirrm %%SITE_PERL%%/%%PERL_ARCH%%/auto/Multiplex/CMD
X@dirrmtry %%SITE_PERL%%/%%PERL_ARCH%%/auto/Multiplex
X@dirrmtry %%SITE_PERL%%/Multiplex
74543a1fcea6ded5caa25840a1853142
echo x - p5-Multiplex-CMD/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >p5-Multiplex-CMD/pkg-descr << 'f6605dae75607ccd4ba836181762ade2'
XThis is a perl module which can be helpful for running some commands for
Xmany hosts.
X
XWWW: http://search.cpan.org/dist/Multiplex-CMD/
X
X--
XAndrey Ignatov
Xrdna@rdna.ru
f6605dae75607ccd4ba836181762ade2
exit
--- p5-Multiplex-CMD.shar ends here ---
